title: chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. graveolens
intro: Chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. graveolens, also known as Wyoming sagebrush, is a subspecies of shrub native to the western United States and Canada. It is a member of the sunflower family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function. Chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. graveolens is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
